reviews.review_description: I love this game so much. However, I’d like to play the mixed reality on my Meta Quest 2. I searched everywhere and I can’t find any way to turn on mixed reality. Plus, the update board in the office didn’t say anything about mixed reality. Has the update been added yet? Idk. What do you think?

developer_response.body: We should have made it more clear that mixed reality is only available for Quest 3 and 3S devices. Sorry about that! Building machines in mixed reality requires an accurate understanding of your environment and unfortunately the Quest 2 cannot provide that.

reviews.review_description: Not fun

developer_response.body: Sorry to hear that! Could you send us an email at hello@metanautlabs.com? We'd love to hear your feedback on how to make our game more fun for players like you.

reviews.review_title: Gorgeous... if this is your thing
reviews.review_description: Did you enjoy setting up series of dominos just to see them fall? Does seeing a set of marbles running down a track sound like fun to you? Then... gadgeteer will likely be "your thing"! Building dominos, seeing them branch out etc. is nice, but setting hundreds of domino stones up, just to see them fall in a minute or so gets tired quickly.  here, of course, all you do is press a button and all dominos are standing again, leaving time to make more corrections and additions. A missing feature is having the rails for balls aligning automatically. There are enough click or magnetic systems in the real world, why should we go for less in the virtual world? Also there is no ball machine, delivering balls at a set rate, for instance to build ball clocks. but the developers are enthusiastic and active... more stuff to come, I am sure!

reviews.review_title: Amazing to use in the classroom!
reviews.review_description: My students love the challenge that Gadgeteer is now bringing to our VR experience. It is awesome to watch them advance in the app and get excited about learning. Great addition to our learning tool belt!

When I for started this game I first noticed the music. How whimsical and playful it sounds. It reminds me of me and my dad playing toys after a long day of kindergarten. Now that he’s gone it brand a little tear to my eye remembering him with this music. 10/10 would recommend.

This game is amazing and really fun to mess around on. My only complaint is that the movement is a weird and some physics are inconsistent. This means that of a block falls, it falls and hits differently almost every time which makes you reset and play the level over and over until it falls right. All of those small things are also not bad and can be worked around. I think there needs to be more levels and possibly more blocks and tools to use. It would also be cool if there was a challenge mode where it made everything a lot harder.

developer_response.body:
Try using joystick locomotion: hold down either of the grip buttons and move either of the joysticks to move around and up/down.

Your suggestions are on our radar and some of them will even be making their way into the game soon!

reviews.review_description: Great Game, smooth easy controls and awesome puzzles! All around fantastic experience. Will recommend to any puzzle lovers ❤
It great and endless it is so much fun!
XKtm

reviews.review_title: Enjoying it a lot, with one minor cavaet
reviews.review_description:
I got this two days ago, and have been enjoying it a lot. The puzzle aspects are fun - solving, winding back and then solving again when the solution doesn't *quite* work as you want.

But the main part is the sandbox area, where I am creating grand masterpieces of machines - rolling balls and going all over the house. However it is here I am finding one problem - that might be just specific to me. My hands tend to shake a little when I am using the controllers, so I am having problems lining up the wooden blocks and metal runners exactly, which means the balls don't run as well as they could - they sometimes stall and stop. And trying to adjust them just makes it worse, because the minor adjustments are harder than the big ones (if you get what I mean). If there could be some kind of snap mode (even if it is optional) it would make life infinitely better - for me at least.

But - that aside - this is a lot of fun, and I see myself enjoying it a great deal for days and weeks and months to come :)

developer_response.body: Track snapping is something we've prototyped before and has worked well for our shaky hands too. We might add this feature in the future.

reviews.review_description: Perfect game if you like 3D VR puzzles

reviews.review_title: One of my first VR purchases - hooked
reviews.review_description:
Hats off to the team that built this 🎩 

Very fun, simple but well executed concept, that's engaging and will surely keep me coming back for more. Can't wait to see what additions the finished version brings to the table!

reviews.review_title: This movement should be the new standard
reviews.review_description: Once you move around in this game you will never want to go back to the hop method. I love how easy it is to rotate and move up and down..... the up and down is fantastic. The gameplay itself reminds me of the incredible machine from back in elementary school. It's just so much fun to come up with new ways to do things
This game is great for when your  bored though I do wish there was an align tool. This would make it a lot easier to make long tracks and tunnels.
The game is absolutely amazing but sandbox mode needs a start/reset button it’s so infuriating to have to reset and start everything manually with the movement controls please add!!!!

developer_response.body: The start/reset button is coming soon!

reviews.review_description: I’m a problem solver at heart and this is game is perfect for my daily need to solve problems or puzzles. Very impressed. I’m upset it wasn’t the first game I purchased.
It has everything puzzle game needs, but the only thing is that it is hard to place things straight.

developer_response.body: Echoing a previous reply for another player, we're working on a feature where players can snap together track gadgets. However, gadget-to-gadget snapping for every gadget is a big feature with lots of complications. A game like Gadgeteer needs to have absolutely the best physics simulation possible and so it'll take us longer to develop this feature. Thanks for the review!

reviews.review_description: We got it and it does not work. Cant get past the main screen when asked for a refund they said no because the refund failed to go back to my paypal account. So stupid.

developer_response.body: Can you confirm you're on the latest version v1.0.17? We had encountered a critical bug that sounds like what you're describing. It has been fixed in the latest update. If it's still a problem, kindly submit a bug report here https://forms.gle/SHFFsaoV3SRmzPKR9
